title: The TCM View of Wealth: Kidney Essence, Earth Element, and Abundance
body:
The Uncomfortable Truth About Your Money & Your Body

You're probably exhausted. You're pushing, you're striving, you're constantly chasing a number that feels perpetually out of reach. And you’re likely doing it all while feeling like you're somehow failing, no matter how much you achieve. You’re telling yourself that if you just work harder, just hustle more, just sacrifice a little bit more of yourself, then finally, finally, you’ll have enough. You’ll be safe. You’ll be abundant. But that feeling never quite arrives, does it? Right?! You feel that?

Truth is, your relationship with money isn't just about financial strategies or budgeting apps. It's not just about what you do. It's about who you are, deeply, fundamentally, in your bones. It’s about your energetic blueprint. It’s about your body. And if you’re always feeling drained, always running on fumes, always operating from a place of scarcity, then your body is already trying to tell you something crucial about your wealth. It's screaming for your attention. Your bank account is a mirror, not a scorecard. And right now, that mirror is reflecting a deeper depletion.

I know this isn't easy to hear. We've been conditioned to believe that money is external, something separate from us. We talk about "making money," as if it's a product we manufacture, rather than an energy we circulate. But what if your financial reality is deeply intertwined with your physical and energetic well-being? What if the struggle you feel with money is literally manifesting as a struggle within your own body?

Today, we're going to dive into a different way of understanding wealth, one that comes from ancient wisdom, specifically Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM). It's a perspective that offers a real lens through which to view your financial health, not as an isolated economic condition, but as an expression of your vital life force. We’re talking about Kidney Essence, the Earth Element, and what true abundance really means when seen through this ancient structure. It's going to challenge everything you thought you knew about money, and honestly, it might just be the most liberating truth you've ever encountered.

Kidney Essence: Your Core Financial Battery

In TCM, the Kidneys aren't just organs that filter blood. They're considered the root of life, the foundation of Yin and Yang in the body. They store what's called "Jing," or Kidney Essence. Think of Jing as your fundamental life battery, your ancestral savings account of vital energy. You're born with a finite amount of this prenatal Jing, inherited from your parents. It's what determines your constitutional strength, your longevity, your reproductive capacity, and even your spiritual vitality.

But here's the kicker: Jing is also your ultimate energetic resource for working through life's challenges. It's the deep reserve you draw upon when you're under stress, when you're pushing boundaries, when you're creating something new. And guess what depletes Jing fastest? Chronic stress, fear, overwork, lack of rest, excessive indulgence, and a constant feeling of scarcity. Sound familiar? Because those are precisely the hallmarks of a toxic money relationship for so many of us.

When you're constantly worried about money, constantly feeling like there isn't enough, constantly pushing yourself to work harder to "catch up" or "get ahead," you're not just depleting your bank account. You're depleting your Jing. You're burning through your core life force. And the symptoms of depleted Jing - chronic fatigue, anxiety, low libido, poor memory, premature aging, feeling ungrounded, a deep sense of fear or insecurity - are often the very symptoms we experience when we're in a financial struggle. It’s not a coincidence. Your body and your finances are speaking the same language.

This isn't about blaming you for being tired. It's about understanding the real connection between your inner state and your outer reality. If your core battery is running low, how can you expect to generate consistent, sustainable external abundance? You can't. It's like trying to run a marathon on an empty stomach. You might make it for a while, but eventually, you'll crash. And that crash often looks like financial setbacks, health crises, or a real sense of burnout and disillusionment.

To cultivate true, sustainable wealth, you must first tend to your Jing. This means prioritizing rest. Deep, restorative rest, not just scrolling through your phone until 2 AM. It means nourishing your body with whole, real foods. It means managing stress, not just by intellectualizing it, but by truly feeling it and moving it through your system. It means cultivating joy and pleasure, because these activities replenish your spirit and, in turn, your Jing. It's a radical act of self-care that directly impacts your capacity for abundance. It's not selfish; it's essential. This isn't just fluffy spiritual talk; it's practical, energetic economics.

The Earth Element: Nourishment, Stability, and Receiving

Next up, let's talk about the Earth Element. In TCM, the Earth Element is associated with the Spleen and Stomach. These organs are responsible for digestion and assimilation - not just of food, but of information, experiences, and emotions. The Earth Element is all about nourishment, stability, grounding, and the capacity to receive. It's about feeling safe, centered, and supported.

When your Earth Element is balanced, you feel nourished. You can easily digest life's challenges. You're able to receive abundance, whether it's love, opportunities, or money, without guilt or resistance. You feel supported by the world around you. You're grounded, steady, and capable of creating and holding wealth. You trust that there's enough, and you feel secure in your ability to access it.

But what happens when your Earth Element is out of whack? Often, it looks like chronic worry. Excessive rumination about money, about the future, about what might go wrong. This worry literally "ties up" the energy of the Spleen, preventing it from doing its job of assimilation and change. You might feel bloated, sluggish, constantly tired after meals, or have digestive issues. Emotionally, you might experience anxiety, insecurity, or an inability to feel truly satisfied, no matter how much you have. You might struggle with setting boundaries, constantly giving your energy away, and then feeling resentful or depleted. You might also struggle with receiving, because deep down, you don't feel worthy of it, or you don't trust that it will last.

Think about this in financial terms. If your Earth Element is weak, you might struggle to "digest" financial information. Budgets feel overwhelming. Investments feel scary. You might hoard money out of fear, or impulsively spend it to temporarily soothe your anxiety. You struggle with a healthy flow - the natural rhythm of giving and receiving. You might be an incredible giver, but completely shut down when it comes to receiving. Know what I mean?

To strengthen your Earth Element and cultivate a healthy relationship with receiving, you need to cultivate practices that bring you back to your center. Grounding exercises. Spending time in nature. Eating regular, nourishing meals mindfully. Learning to say "no" to things that drain you and "yes" to things that nourish you. It's about building trust - trust in yourself, trust in life, and trust in the process of abundance. It's about cultivating a deep sense of inner safety, so you don't constantly feel like you're teetering on the edge of financial collapse, even when you're objectively doing well. This is where Brene Brown's work on vulnerability and worthiness becomes so critical. If you don't feel worthy of receiving, you'll subconsciously push abundance away. Check out her book, The Gifts of Imperfection (paid link), to really dig into that.

Money is Just Energy: Beyond the Numbers

Here's the thing: Money is just energy with a story attached to it. It has no inherent meaning beyond what we assign to it. And the stories we attach to money are often deeply conditioned by our personal history, our family lineage, and societal narratives. These stories dictate how we earn, save, spend, and relate to money. They become our financial operating system, often running in the background, completely unconsciously.

If your story about money is one of struggle, scarcity, fear, or unworthiness, then that's the energy you're constantly putting out, and that's often what you'll attract back. It’s not magic; it’s physics. Energy follows intention, and your unconscious beliefs are powerful intentions. As Krishnamurti said, "The highest form of human intelligence is the ability to observe without evaluating." Can you observe your money story without judgment? Can you see where it's serving you, and where it's holding you back?

Consider your family's relationship with money. Was there always a sense of lack? Were conversations about money shrouded in secrecy or shame? Were there sudden losses or unexpected gains? These experiences, often going back generations, shape our own energetic relationship with wealth. Mark Wolynn's work in It Didn't Start with You (paid link) offers a powerful exploration of how inherited family trauma, including financial trauma, can impact our lives. Our financial patterns aren't just ours; they're often echoes of past generations.

This isn't about blaming your ancestors. It's about becoming aware of the energetic inheritance you've received. Once you're aware, you can choose to transform it. You can consciously rewrite your money story. You can decide that your relationship with money will be different. This is where real power lies - not in chasing external numbers, but in shifting your internal narrative.

Let's sit with the discomfort of that number for a moment. The number in your bank account right now. The number on your debt statement. The number you feel you "should" have. What stories come up when you look at it? What emotions surface? Shame? Fear? Anger? Resignation? Those emotions are clues to your energetic blocks around money. They're telling you where the healing needs to happen.

Cultivating Abundance: More Than Just Cash

Abundance, from a TCM perspective, isn't just about how much money you have in the bank. It's about a rich, vibrant, flowing life force. It's about having enough Jing to live fully. It's about a strong Earth Element that allows you to receive and be nourished. It's about the feeling of being supported, vital, and creatively alive. Money is a part of that, yes, but it's not the whole story. It's a tool, an expression, an amplifier of your underlying energetic state.

When you focus solely on the external acquisition of money without tending to your internal energetic world, it's like building a mansion on a cracked foundation. It might look impressive from the outside, but it's inherently unstable. You might accumulate wealth, but still feel deeply insecure, anxious, or perpetually empty. Lynne Twist, in her book "The Soul of Money," talks about this beautifully - how our core beliefs about scarcity can drive us to accumulate endlessly, yet never feel truly satisfied. True abundance comes from a sense of "enough." It’s an internal state, not an external accumulation.

So, how do you cultivate this deeper form of abundance? It starts with radical self-care. It means treating your body, your energy, and your spirit as your most valuable assets. Because they are. Without them, no amount of money will bring you true peace or joy. It means:

  • Rest: Prioritizing sleep, naps, and periods of true downtime. Not just collapsing from exhaustion, but proactively resting to replenish your Jing.
  • Nourishment: Eating whole, unprocessed foods that support your digestion and energy. Being mindful about *how* you eat.
  • Movement: Engaging in physical activity that feels good and helps circulate your Qi (life force).
  • Mindfulness: Practicing meditation, breathwork, or spending time in nature to calm the mind and strengthen the Earth Element.
  • Boundaries: Learning to protect your energy and say no to things that deplete you.
  • Emotional Processing: Allowing yourself to feel emotions, especially difficult ones, rather than stuffing them down or intellectualizing them. This cleanses your energetic system.
  • Gratitude: Actively appreciating what you already have, which shifts your energetic frequency from scarcity to abundance.
  • Generosity: Giving freely, not from a place of obligation or expectation, but from a full heart. This reinforces the energetic flow of abundance.

This isn't a quick fix. It's a lifelong practice. It's a commitment to your own well-being that will inevitably ripple out and transform your financial reality. Bari Tessler, with her "Art of Money" approach, emphasizes the importance of bringing conscious awareness and emotional intelligence to our financial lives. It's not just about the numbers; it's about the feelings, the stories, the energetic currents underneath it all. Does that land?

Rewriting Your Financial Destiny, Energetically

You have the power to rewrite your financial destiny, not just by changing your external actions, but by transforming your internal energetic world. It requires courage. It requires looking at your deepest fears and insecurities around money. It requires being honest about where you're depleting your Jing and weakening your Earth Element. It requires being vulnerable, not just with others, but with yourself.

This journey isn't about becoming a millionaire overnight (though that might happen too). It's about cultivating a deep, abiding sense of security, vitality, and true abundance within yourself. When you are energetically vibrant, grounded, and open to receiving, money naturally flows to you more easily. Opportunities appear. Resources materialize. Because you are landing with the energy of abundance, rather than the energy of lack.

Think of it as energetic hygiene. Just as you shower to clean your body, you need practices to clean and strengthen your energetic body, especially when it comes to money. We’re often so worried about what we don’t have, we forget to appreciate what we do have: our life force, our health, our capacity for joy. These are the true currencies of abundance.

If you're ready to really commit to this, to integrate your spiritual practice with your financial reality, I highly recommend finding resources that bridge these worlds. Check out Sacred Success (paid link). It’s not just about making more money, it’s about making money in alignment with your deepest values and your energetic well-being. It’s about building a life of true, whole-person wealth.

This isn't about guilt. It's about responsibility. You are responsible for your energy. You are responsible for tending to your Jing and strengthening your Earth Element. And when you do that, you'll find that your outer world, including your financial world, begins to reflect that inner change. It's not magic, it's just how energy works.

FAQ

What exactly is Kidney Essence (Jing) in simple terms?

Think of Kidney Essence, or Jing, as your core life battery or your energetic savings account. You're born with a certain amount, inherited from your parents, which gives you your fundamental vitality, strength, and longevity. It's the deep reserve energy your body uses for growth, development, reproduction, and dealing with stress. When you're constantly stressed, overworking, or not getting enough rest, you're dipping into this core reserve, leading to depletion over time. It's like your ultimate energetic capital that you can't just easily replenish with a good night's sleep; it requires consistent, deep self-care and a mindful approach to how you expend your life force.

How does the Earth Element relate to practical financial habits?

The Earth Element, linked to the Spleen and Stomach, is all about digestion, assimilation, and feeling nourished and stable. In practical financial terms, a balanced Earth Element means you can "digest" financial information without getting overwhelmed. You can create and stick to a budget because you feel grounded and secure, not anxious. You can receive money or opportunities without guilt, because you feel worthy and trust in the flow of abundance. It means you're not constantly worrying about money, which would weaken your Spleen and lead to financial instability or hoarding behaviors. Practicing good financial habits like mindful spending, saving, and investing becomes easier when your Earth Element is strong, as you feel more centered and less prone to impulsive, fear-driven decisions. It allows for a healthy flow of giving and receiving.

Is this approach suggesting I shouldn't work hard for money?

Absolutely not. This approach isn't about avoiding hard work; it's about redefining what "hard work" means and ensuring it's sustainable. It's about working from a place of vitality and conscious intention, rather than from a place of depletion and fear. When your Jing is strong and your Earth Element is balanced, you have more energy, clarity, and resilience to pursue your goals, including financial ones. You can work effectively and passionately without burning out. This perspective encourages smart work, aligned work, and work that honors your body's energetic needs, rather than just endlessly pushing yourself to the brink. It’s about working with your energy, not against it, so that your efforts are more fruitful and less depleting.

What's one small action I can take today to start applying this?

Start small, but be consistent. Today, I challenge you to take one conscious, intentional pause. For just five minutes, close your eyes, put your hand on your belly, and simply breathe. Feel your breath filling your body, grounding you. Don't try to change anything, just observe. This simple act of mindful breathing is a powerful way to connect with your Jing and nourish your Earth Element. It's a tiny step towards reclaiming your energy, shifting your story, and inviting a deeper, more sustainable abundance into your life. Do it now. Don't wait. Your future self, and your bank account, will thank you.

Om Shanti Shanti Shanti
meta_description: Traditional Chinese medicine has a framework for understanding wealth, scarcity, and abundance. It starts with the kidneys and fear.
image_alt: The TCM View of Wealth: Kidney Essence, Earth Element, and Abundance
author: Kalesh
